name: Qalam Education Fund
mission:
Inspiring growth through nurturing education built on a sacred foundation.

We envision a community that thrives off of accessible Islamic knowledge and...
theoryOfChange: Qalam’s approach is to inspire growth through nurturing education built on a sacred foundation, connecting what they impart back to the Holy Qur’an and the Prophetic tradition, with a three-pronged approach: Ta'leem (learning of sacred knowledge), Tarbiya (personal growth and development), and Suhba (mentorship and companionship).
scoreSummary: Qalam Education Fund Inc shows good alignment and moderate impact, with zakat compliance (major risk deductions applied).
zakatClaimEvidence: Dedicated zakat page found at https://www.qalam.institute/zakat (Source: https://www.qalam.institute/zakat)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Context dependent profile. Consider cause fit, risk profile, and evidence maturity before deciding.

keyConcerns.headline: Zakat-collecting organization holds 110 months of reserves
keyConcerns.detail: Zakat must be distributed promptly per Islamic jurisprudence. Large reserves raise concerns about timely distribution.

components.evidence: Explicit zakat program (+4); Muslim-focused organization (+2); Islamic identity (+1)
components.improvement_suggestion: Clarify zakat allocation policy and eligible use categories for donors.
components.name: Muslim Donor Fit

alignment.rationale: Alignment 31/50: MEDIUM Muslim donor fit, education global cause area

components.evidence: No beneficiary data available for cost-per-beneficiary scoring; good program ratio (≥75%) suggests reasonable delivery
components.improvement_suggestion: Publish beneficiary counts consistently so cost-effectiveness can be assessed.
components.name: Cost Per Beneficiary

components.improvement_suggestion: Publish a time-bound plan to deploy excess unrestricted reserves into mission delivery, while keeping a clear operating reserve floor.
components.name: Financial Health

judge_issues.message: The rationale mentions a high program expense ratio and exceptional financial health but doesn't adequately address the significant deductions due to excessive working capital and weak board governance. These factors heavily influenced the impact score and should be explicitly mentioned.

The rationale states that the score is moderated by a focus on capacity building rather than direct poverty relief. While true, it doesn't fully capture the impact of the risk deductions related to board size and excessive reserves, which also contributed to the moderate score.

risks.risk_summary: Key concern: Board too small: 2 members

risks.description: Zakat-collecting organization holds 110 months of reserves — zakat must be distributed promptly

all_citations.claim: The organization reported total revenue of $2,329,468, a program expense ratio of 81.2%, and working capital of 109.7 months.

Financial data including revenue and expenses are documented in the 2023 tax filings.
The organization operates the Qalam Seminary and other educational programs.
Qalam's mission involves nurturing education and community growth through accessible knowledge.
The organization maintains physical facilities to support its educational mission.
Donors have multiple giving methods to support the organization's programs.

dimension_explanations.alignment: Qalam provides a medium fit for donors seeking to support global education and Islamic literacy [5]. The organization is zakat-eligible, allowing donors to fulfill religious obligations through its various educational asnaf categories [2][7].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization focuses on capacity building through its Seminary and Podcast programs, maintaining a high program expense ratio of 81.2% [1][4]. While it shows exceptional financial health with 109.7 months of working capital, the direct cost per beneficiary is not publicly specified [1][3].

baseline_narrative.headline: Qalam Education Fund Inc empowers the Muslim community through accessible Islamic knowledge and nurturing educational programs built on a sacred foundation.

Exceptional fundraising efficiency of $0.01 per $1 raised
Robust working capital of 109.7 months providing long-term financial stability

The organization reported a total revenue of $2,329,468 and maintains a zakat-eligible status to support its educational mission [1][2].

alignment.improvement: The organization could <cite id="2">clarify its specific zakat allocation policy</cite> to provide more transparency on how funds are distributed across asnaf categories.

credibility.explanation: Qalam is an <cite id="3">established institution founded in 2009</cite> with <cite id="3">audited financial statements</cite>. However, credibility is tempered by a <cite id="1">board of only 2 members</cite>, which falls below standard governance recommendations.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the charity should <cite id="1">expand its board of directors</cite> to include more independent members and meet all <cite id="1">BBB Wise Giving Alliance standards</cite>.

impact.explanation: Impact is driven by a <cite id="3">100.0% program ratio</cite> and a <cite id="4">documented theory of change</cite> centered on religious literacy. The charity serves as a <cite id="3">significant grantmaker</cite> for local community initiatives.
impact.improvement: Impact measurement would benefit from <cite id="1">publishing consistent beneficiary counts</cite> to allow for a clear <cite id="1">cost-per-beneficiary analysis</cite>.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to invest in the long-term infrastructure of Islamic education and prefer organizations with extremely low fundraising overhead.
donor_motivations: Want to support the development of future Muslim scholars and leaders
Value high financial efficiency with minimal fundraising costs
Seek to fulfill zakat through educational initiatives (fisabilillah)
Prefer supporting established institutions with a 15+ year track record
giving_considerations: Donations may sit in reserves for long periods due to high working capital
The organization acts as a grantmaker, meaning funds may be passed to other partners
Limited independent board oversight may be a concern for governance-focused donors
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking immediate emergency relief or those who prioritize organizations with large, independent governing boards.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects a verified theory of change but a lack of public data on specific beneficiary outcomes or cost-effectiveness metrics.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: Qalam creates impact by providing structured Islamic education that empowers individuals to serve their communities, supported by digital outreach and intensive retreats.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that educational programs are effectively increasing religious literacy and community engagement.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: The rapid growth in revenue and program expansion indicates a high capacity to absorb new funding for institutional development.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by its <cite id="3">81.2% program expense ratio</cite> and <cite id="3">significant grantmaking activity</cite>.

strengths.detail: The organization operates with a <cite id="3">81.2% program expense ratio</cite> and spends only <cite id="1">$0.01 to raise every dollar</cite>.
Qalam has achieved a <cite id="3">3-year revenue CAGR of 568.6%</cite>, indicating rapid scaling of its <cite id="4">educational programs and seminary</cite>.
The charity holds <cite id="1">109.7 months of working capital</cite>, providing a massive buffer for <cite id="3">long-term institutional stability</cite>.

strengths_deep_dive: Qalam Education Fund demonstrates a unique model of high-efficiency religious education. By maintaining a <cite id="3">81.2% program expense ratio</cite>, the organization ensures that every dollar contributed is technically allocated to its <cite id="4">seminary and intensive programs</cite>. This is supported by <cite id="1">fundraising costs of $0.01 per dollar</cite>, which is far below the industry average. Furthermore, the organization has successfully diversified its impact by becoming a grantmaker, providing <cite id="3">$414,289 to domestic recipients</cite> to strengthen the broader Muslim educational ecosystem.
rich_narrative.summary: Qalam Education Fund Inc is a Texas-based non-profit dedicated to <cite id="4">providing accessible Islamic knowledge</cite> through a sacred foundation. Since its <cite id="3">founding in 2009</cite>, the organization has evolved into a multi-faceted educational institution. It operates the <cite id="4">Qalam Seminary, Qur’an Intensives, and digital podcasts</cite> to nurture community growth. The charity reported <cite id="3">$2,338,320 in total revenue</cite> for the 2023 tax year, reflecting a significant <cite id="3">3-year revenue CAGR of 568.6%</cite>. This rapid growth highlights a surging demand for its <cite id="5">educational and retreat programs</cite>. Qalam maintains a <cite id="3">81.2% program expense ratio</cite> according to its latest filings, ensuring that donor funds are directed toward its core mission of <cite id="6">religious instruction and community service</cite>. The organization also serves as a grantmaker, distributing <cite id="3">$414,289 in domestic grants</cite> to partner organizations like Roots Community Inc and the Qalam Seminary. While the charity demonstrates <cite id="1">exceptional fundraising efficiency of $0.01 per $1 raised</cite>, it maintains an unusually high <cite id="1">working capital of 109.7 months</cite>. This suggests a very conservative financial posture with significant reserves available for future expansion or institutional stability.
